sections.paragraph:
καταρρέω impf. κατέρρεον; fut. 3 pl. καταρρεύσουσιν Jer 13:17 Sym.; aor. 3 pl. κατέρρευσαν Is 64:1 Aq; pf. 3 sg. κατερρύηκεν Jer 8:13 (s. ῥέω; Hom. et al.; PMagd 24, 5 [III b.c.]; LXX; TestSol 17:3; JosAs 29:1 cod. A for ἐρρύη; ApcMos 20; Joseph.) flow down of pus (Horapollo 2, 57) and foulness ApcPt 11:26.—DELG s.v. ῥέω

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. pl. = plural

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

aor. aor. = aorist

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

  1. pf. = perfect
  2. sg. = singular

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PMagd PMagd = Papyrus de Magdôla—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Horapollo Horapollo , IV/V? a.d.—List 5

ApcPt ApcPt = Apocalypse of Peter—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 526
